content_markdown: Which of the following is **not** an assumption on which the kinetic model of an ideal gas is based?

diagram_metadata.description: No diagram present in the question.

options.label: A
options.text: All molecules behave as if they are perfectly elastic spheres.

options.label: B
options.text: The mean-square speed of the molecules is proportional to the kelvin temperature.

options.label: C
options.text: Unless in contact, the forces between molecules are negligible.

options.label: D
options.text: The molecules are in continuous random motion.

vibe_explanation: The kinetic theory of gases is built on specific postulates (assumptions) like elastic collisions, negligible intermolecular forces, and random motion. While the relationship between mean-square speed and temperature is a mathematical consequence derived from these assumptions, it is a result of the model rather than an initial assumption itself.
